name: Apple Ads: Portal Campaign/Adgroup List
summary: List Apple Ads campaigns for the signed-in org, or ad groups inside a specific campaign.
description: List Apple Ads campaigns for the signed-in org, or list ad groups inside a specific campaign when `campaign_id` is supplied. Read-only DOM extraction against the authenticated portal. Does NOT return metrics — Apple's portal renders performance numbers in shadow-DOM cells, so for metrics use the portal's data-export API or a same-origin-request workflow.

campaign_id.description: Drill into this campaign and list its ad groups instead of listing campaigns.

org_id.description: Apple Ads org id. If omitted, uses whichever org the portal lands on for the user.

steps.name: Open the portal in a fresh tab
Navigate to campaigns or campaign-detail view
Wait for campaign or ad-group anchor

help.summary: List Apple Ads campaigns for the signed-in org, or ad groups inside a specific campaign.

parameters.campaign_id: Drill into this campaign and list its ad groups instead of listing campaigns.
parameters.org_id: Apple Ads org id. If omitted, uses whichever org the portal lands on for the user.

examples.description: List campaigns for the signed-in org.

returns: Array of { name, href, campaign_id?, adgroup_id? } rows. Each campaign appears multiple times because the portal grid renders the campaign name + each metric cell as its own anchor; dedupe by `campaign_id` or `adgroup_id` downstream.
notes: Does NOT return spend/impression/tap/install metrics — those are inside shadow DOM cells and aren't reliably extractable. For metrics, hit Apple's reporting API directly via a same-origin-request workflow.
Apple removed tab-based report switching in early 2026; the old `report_type` param has been dropped.

Each row is repeated per metric anchor (~10× per campaign). Dedupe by campaign_id/adgroup_id downstream — the first occurrence of each id usually carries the entity name, the rest carry metric strings like '$0.00' / '0%'.
